features.title: Bootstrap minimal AI agent harness template
features.description: Create the harness state files, schema, prompts, validation scripts, and tiny example needed to prove the template works end to end.
acceptance: ./init.sh validates required files and feature_list.json
scripts/validate-feature.sh F001 validates the selected feature
scripts/summarize-progress.sh prints feature totals and next work
examples/tiny-cli test coverage passes without third-party dependencies

features.title: Add vendor-neutral lightweight orchestrator
features.description: Add a generic orchestrator that follows the startup protocol, selects one unfinished feature, previews or runs Coding Agent and Evaluator Agent prompts, supports eval-only mode, and keeps vendor-specific agent execution configurable.
acceptance: orchestrator.py exists at the template root
python3 orchestrator.py --dry-run runs the startup protocol and exits successfully when there is no unfinished feature
python3 orchestrator.py --eval-only F001 --dry-run previews the evaluator prompt
./init.sh validates orchestrator.py syntax
README.md and SPEC.md document the orchestrator boundary

features.title: Adopt practiced AGENTS guardrails
features.description: Refine the template AGENTS.md using proven guardrails from the production agent workflow, including initializer responsibilities, state safety rules, external behavior verification, external tool schema rules, work rules, anti-patterns, and recoverability goals.
acceptance: AGENTS.md includes an Initializer role for bootstrapping repository state
AGENTS.md includes state safety rules for preserving feature_list.json fields and ordering
AGENTS.md includes external behavior verification rules for CLI, API, runtime, and structured tool output assumptions
AGENTS.md includes anti-patterns that prevent premature completion and chat-history reliance
./init.sh passes after the guardrail update

features.title: Add testing layers to the harness template
features.description: Add an explicit test plan and dependency-free unit, contract, and smoke test layers to the template, wire them into ./init.sh, and document how downstream projects should extend the layers.
acceptance: test_plan.md documents the verification entry point and unit, contract, harness, and smoke layers
test/unit contains tests for validation and progress helper behavior
test/contract contains tests that lock AGENTS, feature schema, prompts, and orchestrator contracts
test/smoke contains an end-to-end smoke test for the template commands
./init.sh runs unit, contract, smoke, and optional harness tests

features.title: Strengthen contract tests for AI harness obligations
features.description: Refine contract tests so they verify AI agent obligations and harness boundaries, including external behavior verification, feature state preservation, prompt restrictions, evaluator anti-premature-completion rules, and orchestrator state-transition contracts.
acceptance: Contract tests verify AGENTS external behavior verification requirements beyond section presence
Contract tests verify feature state fields, uniqueness, done/pass consistency, and unknown-field preservation requirements
Contract tests verify work, continue, and evaluate prompts constrain AI behavior and state mutation
Contract tests verify orchestrator state-transition and eval-only boundaries by static inspection
./init.sh passes after strengthening contract coverage

features.title: Replace generic agent command with role adapters
features.description: Remove the generic HARNESS_AGENT_COMMAND orchestration hook and make orchestrator.py dispatch explicit Coding Agent and Evaluator Agent role adapters instead.
acceptance: orchestrator.py no longer references HARNESS_AGENT_COMMAND
orchestrator.py dispatches scripts/run-coding-agent.sh for Coding Agent prompts
orchestrator.py dispatches scripts/run-evaluator-agent.sh for Evaluator Agent prompts
README.md, SPEC.md, and AGENTS.md document role adapters instead of a generic agent command
./init.sh passes after the adapter boundary change

features.title: Add repository knowledge map, quality rubric, and run artifacts
features.description: Add a docs knowledge map, evaluator quality rubric, and run artifact mechanism so the harness captures repository-local knowledge, evaluator criteria, and per-run evidence without bloating AGENTS.md or progress.md.
acceptance: docs/README.md indexes architecture, testing, external behavior, workflow, and decision-log documents
QUALITY.md defines evaluator rubric criteria and pass/fail guidance
runs/ contains a run log template and scripts/summarize-runs.sh summarizes recorded runs
AGENTS.md points agents to docs/ and QUALITY.md instead of becoming the only knowledge source
Evaluator prompt requires applying QUALITY.md and recording run evidence when appropriate
./init.sh validates the new knowledge, quality, and run artifact files

features.title: Add failure-domain to harness-improvement loop
features.description: Add a mechanism that classifies failures by domain and forces failed or blocked run records to assess whether the harness itself needs improvement through docs, prompts, scripts, schemas, tests, or follow-up features.
acceptance: docs/failure-domains.md defines failure domains and the improvement loop
runs/RUN_TEMPLATE.md includes Failure Analysis fields for failure domain, summary, harness improvement, and follow-up feature
scripts/check-failure-domains.sh fails when failed run records omit domain or harness improvement assessment
orchestrator.py writes a failed run record when unattended coding or evaluation fails
AGENTS.md and prompts require agents to classify failures and turn harness weaknesses into durable improvements
./init.sh runs the failure-domain check
Contract, unit, and smoke tests cover the failure-domain mechanism

features.title: Add Go server example
features.description: Add a dependency-free Go HTTP server example that demonstrates how the harness can validate service-style projects with standard library tests.
acceptance: examples/go-server contains a Go module with a small HTTP server
The server exposes GET /healthz and GET /greet?name=Codex endpoints
The Go server example has tests covering health, greeting, blank-name defaulting, and method rejection
./init.sh verifies the Go server example with go test ./... when Go is installed
README.md documents how to test and run the Go server example

features.title: Add Makefile and GitHub Actions CI
features.description: Add a Makefile that standardizes local harness commands and a GitHub Actions workflow that runs the same CI verification path on push and pull request.
acceptance: Makefile defines init, test, validate, unit, contract, smoke, go-example, dry-run, summarize, and ci targets
make ci runs ./init.sh, feature validation, and orchestrator dry-run
.github/workflows/ci.yml runs make ci on push, pull_request, and workflow_dispatch
README.md documents Makefile usage
./init.sh validates the Makefile and GitHub Actions workflow are present

features.title: Explore concurrent agent execution
features.description: If future workflows need higher throughput, explore a bounded concurrent-agent mode that can run independent Coding Agent or Evaluator Agent work without violating feature state safety, run evidence, or evaluator gating.
acceptance: Concurrency design defines which feature states may run in parallel and which must remain serialized
Orchestrator concurrency preserves one-writer semantics for feature_list.json and progress.md
Run records make concurrent work attributable to a feature, agent role, and attempt
Evaluator gating remains per feature and cannot be bypassed by concurrent execution
Tests cover race-prone state transitions before concurrent mode is enabled

features.title: Improve README positioning and onboarding
features.description: Rewrite the README first screen and quick start so new users understand the project as a repository-level harness for resumable AI coding rather than a prompt collection.
acceptance: README opens with Make AI coding projects resumable
README explains the concrete failure modes: interrupted sessions, long context, quota exhaustion, forgotten decisions, unrelated edits, and premature completion
README states that the project is not a prompt collection
README separates Use This Template from Verify This Repository
README explains that the orchestrator is intentionally boring and only enforces workflow boundaries
Contract tests lock the README positioning and onboarding language

features.title: Add OSS readiness files and repository metadata
features.description: Add public OSS readiness files and repository metadata so the project is easier to discover, evaluate, contribute to, and maintain.
acceptance: LICENSE, CONTRIBUTING.md, SECURITY.md, and CHANGELOG.md are present
GitHub issue templates exist for bug reports and feature requests
./init.sh validates the OSS readiness files are present
Contract tests verify OSS readiness files and issue templates
GitHub repository description and topics are set for discoverability

features.title: Add clean-state reset command
features.description: Add a make clean workflow that resets project-specific harness state after copying the template into a new project while preserving the harness files, docs, scripts, tests, examples, and run template.
acceptance: Makefile defines a clean target that calls scripts/clean-state.py
scripts/clean-state.py resets feature_list.json to an empty features array
scripts/clean-state.py resets progress.md to a fresh-project state
scripts/clean-state.py removes recorded run artifacts while preserving runs/RUN_TEMPLATE.md and runs/.gitkeep
README.md documents running make clean after copying the template
./init.sh validates scripts/clean-state.py is present
Unit and contract tests cover clean-state reset behavior

features.title: Document real-world usage references
features.description: Document the real projects that informed the harness design and link them from README and the repository knowledge map without copying full project code into examples.
acceptance: docs/real-world-usage.md links home-guard-tg and agent-remote-tg
docs/real-world-usage.md explains the harness lessons extracted from those projects
README.md links docs/real-world-usage.md and both real project repositories
docs/README.md indexes real-world-usage.md
./init.sh validates docs/real-world-usage.md is present
Contract tests cover real-world usage references

features.title: Link announcement article from README
features.description: Link the public announcement article from README so visitors can understand the story and motivation behind the harness.
acceptance: README.md includes an Announcement section near the top
README.md links the public announcement article
Contract tests verify the announcement link remains present

features.title: Add AI Agent Harness skill
features.description: Add a distributable skill that helps users initialize or repair the harness in a project, follow planning, one-feature implementation, evaluation, and explicit finalize-and-commit workflows while preserving repository files as the durable source of state.
acceptance: skills/ai-agent-harness/SKILL.md defines the skill trigger and keeps repository state as the durable source of truth
The skill includes a deterministic initializer that supports new, adopt, repair, and check modes without overwriting conflicting project files by default
The skill documents planning, one-feature work, evaluator verification, and explicit finalize-and-commit workflows
The finalize-and-commit workflow requires explicit user satisfaction before staging or committing
README.md and SPEC.md document the skill as a convenience layer that preserves the vendor-neutral harness protocol
./init.sh validates the skill files and tests cover the skill contract
